grant_title: Maine Senior Assistance Programs, Benefits, and Grants (2026)
eligible_applicant_type: Low-income older adults (earning less than 200% of federal poverty level)
geographic_restrictions: Maine residents only, with priority for rural and underserved areas
project_area: Senior assistance programs including property tax relief, healthcare coverage, in-home care, food assistance, heating aid, housing support, legal services, and transportation services for older adults in Maine
demographic_requirements: Priority given to seniors aged 65+, disabled individuals, veterans, low-income households (below 200% FPL), and those facing housing or heating crises. Specific programs have additional age, income, or asset limits.
eligibility_summary: Eligibility varies by program but generally requires Maine residency, age 60+ (or disability), income and asset limits, and specific needs (e.g., nursing home level of care for HCBS Waiver). Programs include Property Tax Fairness Credit, HCBS Waiver, HEAP heating assistance, SNAP food benefits, MaineCare healthcare, and veterans' benefits. Many programs have waiting lists, especially in rural areas.
